title: browse-web execute-task capability_mismatch
summary: Agent tried to extract content from a dynamically rendered page using a tool that only captures static HTML. Switching to a web-capable route resolved the issue.

evidence.desired_outcome: Extract main content from a public webpage.
evidence.symptom: The page opened, but the extracted content was incomplete. Only the initial HTML shell was captured.
evidence.context: The target page appears to rely on client-side JavaScript rendering to populate its main content area.
evidence.failed_step: DOM content extraction returned empty main content area
reproduction_steps: Used browser-cdp skill to open the page
Extracted HTML content — only static shell captured
Retried extraction without changing the rendering strategy

inference.why_current_path_failed: The current tool only captures static HTML. The page requires client-side JavaScript rendering to populate its content, which this tool does not execute.
inference.best_candidate_route_detail: Switch to a browser path with stronger dynamic rendering support. playwright-mcp is a good candidate for this task as it can execute JavaScript and interact with the rendered DOM. If the page is simpler, web_fetch may suffice for static content.

resolution.follow_up_notes: Switched to playwright-mcp which successfully extracted the dynamic content. Trade-off: higher setup cost compared to the original skill.
